reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku, but tool fetched general Akita spots. No sakura-specific filtering applied; results include castles, volcanoes, and fireworks with no cherry blossom season information. Misses Kakunodate and Hirosaki entirely.
missing_data: Dedicated sakura_meisho spots in Tohoku (Kakunodate, Hirosaki, Kitakami Tenshochi); season/bloom timing; no cherry blossom kind filter applied across Tohoku prefectures.
improvement_hint: Apply kind filter for sakura_meisho_100 across multiple Tohoku prefectures; add bloom timing metadata; route tool through a seasonal intent classifier to surface flower-viewing relevant spots.

reasoning: Response correctly surfaces Niigata sake brands (越の三梅, 越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田) with Wikidata QIDs and MAFF GI items. English names provided. Minor gap: no brewery addresses or visiting info, and GI items shift to food rather than brewery venues.
missing_data: Brewery physical addresses and visiting hours; Hakkaisan Brewery tourism program; sake tourism experience venues beyond brand listings.
improvement_hint: Add brewery_visit kind tag to wikidata brewery entities and surface visiting_hours/address when available; link MAFF sake GI to physical brewery locations.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for ski resorts with onsen in Hakuba. Response surfaces Happo-One correctly, but includes unrelated items (UN Tourism village news, alpine lake, art museum) with no onsen data. Onsen kind tags absent for Hakuba area.
missing_data: Onsen facilities in Hakuba (Mimizuku-no-Yu, Happo-One onsen); onsen_kind tags on spots; co-located ski+onsen package information.
improvement_hint: Add onsen and hot_spring kind tags to Hakuba spots; enable compound constraint matching (ski_resort AND onsen) at query time rather than returning all spots by municipality.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage in Kansai region, but tool only fetched Kyoto spots with no koyo filter or season context. Results include Nijo Castle and Toji with no foliage-specific data. Osaka, Nara, Hyogo koyo spots entirely absent.
missing_data: Kansai-wide coverage (Osaka, Nara, Hyogo); koyo_meisho kind tags; autumn foliage season information and peak timing.
improvement_hint: Expand query to multiple Kansai prefectures; add koyo_meisho kind filter; include seasonal peak-week metadata for foliage spots in response.

reasoning: Query asks for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool fetched Nara spots, which includes Yoshino-yama with sakura_meisho_100 tag prominently ranked first — an excellent match. Other spots are general Nara attractions but the top result directly addresses intent.
missing_data: Osaka and Hyogo cherry blossom spots (Osaka Castle Park, Himeji Castle); bloom timing and hanami logistics.
improvement_hint: Fan out to multiple Kansai prefectures when region-level cherry blossom intent is detected; surface bloom calendar data alongside sakura_meisho results.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on Okinawa's remote islands. Tool returned general Okinawa spots dominated by Naha castles and historical sites. No dark-sky, stargazing, or remote island (Iriomote, Yonaguni, Kerama) data present. Completely misses the constraint.
missing_data: Iriomote, Yonaguni, Kerama islands stargazing data; dark_sky kind tags; Iriomote being Japan's only designated Dark Sky Park; island ferry access info.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky and star_viewing kind tags; create island-specific sub-prefecture routing for Okinawan remote islands; tag Iriomote as dark sky sanctuary.

reasoning: Query asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returns multiple hito_yu_kai (secret hot spring) ryokan with addresses confirming rural locations (Shiraoi, Yakumo, Kamifurano, Asahidake). Confirmed designation adds credibility. Minor: coordinates null for all ryokan.
missing_data: GPS coordinates for ryokan; price range; booking URLs beyond hito-yu reference pages; seasonal accessibility (snow road closure info).
improvement_hint: Geocode hito_yu_kai entries against address strings; add booking_url field; flag winter road-closure risk for mountain onsen addresses in Hokkaido.

reasoning: Query asks for islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool fetched Kagawa spots but the response is dominated by mainland attractions (Ritsurin Garden, Manno Lake, Marugame Castle). Naoshima, Shodoshima, and Teshima islands are entirely absent from the visible results.
missing_data: Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Megijima islands; art island (Setouchi Triennale) context; ferry access information.
improvement_hint: Add island kind tag to Seto Inland Sea islands and enable island-specific filtering; ensure Naoshima/Shodoshima are tagged as Setouchi and rank highly for island queries.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for indigo dyeing workshops. Tool returns 3 METI crafts for Tokushima: one directly related (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave using indigo), one pottery (Otani Ware), and one washi paper. Response identifies indigo-textile craft but provides no workshop/venue information for actual dyeing experiences.
missing_data: Workshop venue names and addresses for indigo dyeing experiences; Aigamo-no-Sato or Ai-no-Yakata type venues; experience booking information.
improvement_hint: Add experience_workshop kind tag to spots near craft production areas; link METI craft designations to known workshop venues in the same municipality.

reasoning: Query asks for shukubo (temple lodging) on Mt. Koya. Tool returns multiple confirmed Koyasan Shukubo Association members with phone numbers, descriptions, and source URLs. Directly and comprehensively addresses the intent. Excellent result.
missing_data: Coordinates for shukubo entries; English names; price ranges; booking URLs; whether zazen/shojin-ryori are offered at each.
improvement_hint: Add English name translations for shukubo entries; geocode from address data; add amenity tags (zazen_available, shojin_ryori) to differentiate shukubo options for agent reasoning.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 153 matches including Shimane Buke Yashiki, Tokyo gate, Fukui Uchiyama family, and Kakunodate with rich description. Good diversity. Tokyo result slightly undermines 'outside Kyoto' spirit but is informative.
missing_data: Chiran (Kagoshima) samurai district; Kitsuki (Oita) samurai town; explicit filtering to exclude Tokyo/Kyoto results when 'off-beaten-path' intent detected.
improvement_hint: Add region exclusion hint when query says 'outside X'; boost preservation_district + buke_yashiki compound results; add description for Kakunodate weeping cherry context already in corpus.

reasoning: Query asks for summer festivals in Akita. Tool returns only 3 intangible folk art designations — Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shrine Festival — all from bunka_intangible source. Misses the iconic Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり), Omagari Fireworks and Noshiro Fire Festival. The 3-item count is very thin.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり); Omagari National Fireworks Competition; Noshiro Fire Festival; summer festival dates; festival schedule data.
improvement_hint: Expand festival coverage by including scraped_schema_event and get_events (SPARQL live) sources alongside bunka_intangible; ensure Kanto Festival has proper summer tagging; add date field to all festival entries.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in the San'in region. Tool returns Shimane spots dominated by Matsue Castle, Lake Shinji, Iwami Silver Mine, and Mount Sanbe. No fishing village (漁港 machi) data present. Misses Ama (Oki Islands), Yunotsu, Hagi-area fishing communities.
missing_data: Oki Islands fishing villages; Yunotsu port town; Ama fishing community (海士町); fishing_port kind tags for coastal settlements.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port and fishing_village kind tags to coastal municipalities; implement Sanin region fan-out including Tottori coast; create lifestyle/ruraltourism spot category.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. Response lists 8 METI crafts including Mino Washi (美濃和紙) which is the correct answer, but it's buried among other crafts (Mino Ware ceramics, lacquerware, wood carving). No workshop venue or experience location is provided.
missing_data: Mino Washi Akari Art Museum address; Udatsu Washi Paper Village experience venue; booking information for hands-on washi making workshops.
improvement_hint: Surface Mino Washi more prominently when washi query detected; link METI craft production areas to experience venues in the same municipality; add experience_available flag.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool fetches Ishikawa prefecture-wide spots dominated by Kanazawa (castle, Kenroku-en, shrine). Noto Peninsula fishing villages (Wajima, Suzu, Sosogi Coast) are entirely absent from visible results.
missing_data: Wajima fishing port and morning market; Sosogi Coast; Suzu salt fields; Noto-specific municipality routing; fishing_village kind tags.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. Response includes Tottori (expected) plus Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari dune museum (Hokkaido), Fukigehama (Kagoshima) across multiple prefectures. Good geographic diversity. 91 matches is strong.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu); Sarufutsu (Hokkaido) dune coast; description text missing for some entries.
improvement_hint: Add descriptive text to sand_dune kind results that lack description_en; surface Nakatajima as a notable lesser-known alternative; ensure 'beyond X' framing triggers exclusion or de-prioritization of the named location.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional crafts in San'in (Shimane). Response returns 4 METI-designated crafts in Chinese: Iwami Ware ceramics, Sekishu Washi paper, Unshū abacus, and Izumo stone lanterns — all Shimane-specific with high-quality descriptions. Excellent coverage and translation quality.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts for full San'in coverage; craft experience venues; pottery making workshop locations.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields beyond Furano. Tool returns general Hokkaido spots (Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle). No lavender or flower field data present in visible results. The lavender kind tag was added in earlier iterations but doesn't surface here.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional old towns (古い町並み) in Kyushu, but in Tagalog specifically mentions 'traditional coffee towns' — likely a translation artifact for 'traditional towns'. Tool fetches Oita spots: Usuki Castle, Usa Shrine, Oka Castle. No preserved historic streetscape (伝建地区) data for Kyushu present.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (old farmhouse) stays in Hokuriku. Tool returns Ishikawa hotels: hito_yu_kai onsen ryokan (not kominka), and a 'Student housing 1' hostel — a completely irrelevant and potentially fabricated entity name. No kominka lodging data present.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage returns Kongōbu-ji, Kumano Kodo (UNESCO WHS), Kumano Sanzan — exactly the right content for Kii Peninsula mountain religious pilgrimage. Tool scope note helpfully points to additional tools for UNESCO. Excellent.

reasoning: Query asks for fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns 129 Niigata events with 長岡まつり大花火大会 present but buried third after spring flower festivals. The fireworks query should surface hanabi events first. Results are un-filtered for summer/fireworks.

reasoning: Query asks for bamboo crafts in Oita. Response returns exactly 2 crafts: Hakata Ori weave (multi-prefecture including Oita) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts — the latter being precisely the intended answer with rich description of 8 weaving techniques and Madake bamboo. Excellent targeting.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes in rural Shikoku. Tool returns Ehime spots including Omishima (cycling_route tag) which is directly relevant as part of Shimanami Kaido. However, other cycling-tagged spots in Kochi, Tokushima, and Kagawa are absent. Coverage across Shikoku is sparse.

The response provides art classification, not venue guidance — no theater addresses or performance schedules.
